briefing: Markets sold off sharply today with NASDAQ down 2.3% and S&P 500 down 1.4%, a clear risk-off signal. No new candidates were screened, so no buys were made; existing positions were held since a broad market pullback alone isn't a reason to exit strong holdings.

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors broadly sell riskier assets like growth stocks and move toward safety, often triggered by economic worries or uncertainty. On days like this, it's usually better to hold steady with existing positions and avoid chasing new trades until clearer signals emerge.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「風險趨避」(Risk-Off)是指投資人普遍拋售成長股等高風險資產,轉向較安全的資產,通常由經濟疑慮或不確定性引發。在這類日子裡,通常最好是按兵不動、持有現有部位,避免在訊號不明朗時追加新交易。

market_education.summary_en: Both major indices fell together today, with NASDAQ's steeper 2.3% drop versus the S&P 500's 1.4% decline suggesting growth and tech-heavy names were sold off harder than the broader market. This pattern typically reflects a shift toward risk aversion, where investors rotate out of higher-beta stocks. No specific news catalysts were provided in today's data feed to attribute the move to a single cause.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日大盤同步下跌,納斯達克指數重挫2.3%,跌幅明顯大於標普500指數的1.4%,顯示科技股與高成長股遭到更大力度的拋售。這種走勢通常反映市場風險偏好降溫,資金從高波動性股票撤出。今日數據中未提供具體新聞事件可歸因於此次下跌。
